level_signals: 7+ years of experience in technical program management or related roles
Expected to drive executive-level communication and governance
Owns end-to-end cross-functional program execution across hardware lifecycle

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience in technical program management, hardware operations, manufacturing operations, infrastructure deployment, or related technical execution roles.
Experience supporting hardware systems at scale, ideally including servers, racks, networking hardware, data center infrastructure, or high-performance compute environments.
Strong understanding of hardware development and deployment lifecycle, including NPI, qualification, manufacturing ramp, logistics, installation, validation, and operational support.
Demonstrated ability to manage complex cross-functional schedules, dependencies, risks, and executive communications.
Strong technical fluency across hardware systems, rack integration, manufacturing readiness, and infrastructure deployment.
Proven ability to operate in ambiguous environments and create scalable execution mechanisms.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, operations, supply chain, or equivalent practical experience.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with AI infrastructure, hyperscale data centers, cloud infrastructure, or high-density compute systems is a plus.
Experience with GPU, accelerator, server, rack, or cluster-scale infrastructure programs.
Background in hardware operations, manufacturing program management, supply chain operations, data center deployment, or technical infrastructure TPM.
Familiarity with rack integration, power/cooling constraints, cabling, networking, serviceability, and deployment readiness.
Experience building operating rhythms, readiness reviews, risk registers, launch dashboards, or executive program reviews.
Experience scaling programs from prototype/NPI into repeatable production deployment.

scope.surface: AI infrastructure hardware programs spanning servers, racks, networking hardware, power/cooling, and data center infrastructure (Stargate team)
scope.business_model: internal infrastructure / hyperscale AI compute deployment
primary_metrics_inferred: Hardware operational health and repair performance
Execution efficiency across hardware lifecycle milestones
core_responsibilities: Drive end-to-end Hardware Operations readiness programs across AI infrastructure systems
Develop and operationalize scalable hardware operations processes, workflows, and support models
Lead cross-functional execution of Hardware Operations readiness initiatives
Partner across engineering, manufacturing, supply chain, and operations teams for alignment on readiness milestones
Develop operational scorecards, reporting frameworks, and metric algorithms
Identify operational, technical, supplier, tooling, and process risks; drive mitigation plans
Lead cross-functional issue resolution during hardware deployment, validation, and sustaining operations
Create and mature operational governance models including readiness reviews and escalation management
Ensure operational knowledge sharing across internal teams, suppliers, and infrastructure partners

green_flags: Clear team context (Stargate) and well-defined product surface for hardware infrastructure
Role spans full hardware lifecycle from NPI through sustaining operations — broad scope
OpenAI is a high-profile employer with significant investment in infrastructure (Stargate program)
Explicit list of cross-functional partners gives strong sense of organizational context
JD encourages candidates who 'thrive' with a nuanced soft-skills section, suggesting culture fit matters
red_flags: No location specified anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite; no cities listed
No compensation or equity information provided
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information stated
Posting date unknown — cannot assess freshness
Very broad scope may indicate under-defined team structure or high-pressure execution environment
